hero.title: 4 Days, The Navagraha Temples.
hero.titleAccent: The Nine Planetary Temples of Tamil Nadu.
hero.intro: Nine ancient temples, each dedicated to one of the celestial bodies, scattered around the temple heartland near Kumbakonam. The Navagraha pilgrimage is among the most sacred journeys in Tamil tradition.

overview.heading: 4 Days Navagraha Temples Tour: The Full Picture
body: The Navagraha temples are nine ancient shrines in Tamil Nadu, each devoted to one of the nine celestial bodies of Vedic astrology: the Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, and the lunar nodes Rahu and Ketu. Pilgrims visit them to seek balance and blessings according to their birth chart.

This 4 day Navagraha temples package from Malaysia is arranged privately so you can complete the circuit comfortably, mostly around Kumbakonam in the fertile Cauvery delta. A knowledgeable driver guide handles the routing and explains the significance of each temple and its associated planet.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Nine Planetary Shrines
items.body: Each temple is dedicated to one graha (celestial body), with its own rituals, offerings and remedies. Completing the circuit is believed to balance planetary influences in one's life.

items.title: The Cauvery Delta Temple Belt
items.body: Most Navagraha temples cluster around Kumbakonam and Thanjavur, a region dense with ancient Chola temples and rich agricultural beauty.

items.title: Astrology & Ritual Guidance
items.body: Your guide helps you understand the significance of each shrine and the traditional offerings, making the pilgrimage meaningful rather than just a series of stops.

days.title: Arrive Trichy / Chennai
days.description: Met at the airport and driven to Kumbakonam, the base for the Navagraha circuit.

days.title: Navagraha Temples (Part 1)
days.description: Visit the first set of planetary temples around the delta.

days.title: Navagraha Temples (Part 2)
days.description: Complete the planetary circuit with the remaining shrines.

days.title: Depart
days.description: A final temple or visit to Thanjavur's Brihadeeswarar Temple before your airport transfer.

whyBook.body: The Navagraha temples are spread across the delta, and the order and timing of visits matter to many pilgrims. We handle the routing and pair you with a guide who understands the tradition. Dhesu has arranged South India pilgrimages since 1988.

sections.heading: Navagraha Temples Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The Navagraha pilgrimage is one of the most distinctive temple journeys in South India, drawing devotees who follow Vedic astrology. Visiting all nine planetary temples is believed to bring balance and blessings.

This private package handles the routing across the Cauvery delta and pairs you with a guide who explains the significance and rituals of each shrine, making the circuit smooth and meaningful.

The region also holds the magnificent Chola temples of Thanjavur, which many travellers add on.

sections.heading: The Significance of the Navagraha
sections.body:
In Vedic tradition, the nine grahas (celestial bodies) are believed to influence human life. Each Navagraha temple is dedicated to one, with specific offerings and remedies associated with it.

Pilgrims often visit based on their birth chart, seeking to strengthen favourable influences and remedy difficult ones. Your guide can explain the customary offerings at each temple.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Tiruchirappalli or Chennai Airport (TRZ / MAA). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for the Navagraha tour?
items.answer: November to February is the most comfortable, avoiding the intense heat of the Tamil Nadu summer. The temples are open year-round, with festival days drawing larger crowds.

items.question: Do I need to know my birth chart?
items.answer: It helps if you want to focus on specific planetary remedies, but it is not required. Many pilgrims simply visit all nine temples for overall blessings. Your guide can explain the customs at each.

items.question: Is there a specific order to visit the temples?
items.answer: Traditionally, pilgrims follow a particular sequence, often starting with the Sun temple. We route the trip to make the circuit efficient while respecting the customary order as much as possible.
